page: Hetzner Cloud cx33 is an x86_64 virtual server powered by the QEMU hypervisor. It features 4 shared vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 4 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache. The server is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core, and includes 80 GB of local SSD storage. No GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark evaluations show strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth (470.59 GB/sec read-only cached, 67.66 GB/sec uncached), and static web serving throughput (6.15 GB/sec) fall within the average range. However, the server exhibits weaker performance in software compilation with Clang and in PassMark memory tests, both scoring in the bottom 25%. Large-block memory write speeds reach 49.67 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations average 7.84 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the cx33 offers a low-cost entry point for multi-core x86 workloads, leveraging shared AMD EPYC resources to keep resource density high relative to cost. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability under sustained, concurrent neighbor workloads, alongside the lower memory-to-vCPU ratio. This server is appropriate for lightweight web serving, basic database operations, single-threaded text or image processing, and small-scale development environments that do not require dedicated CPU cores or high memory capacity.
description: Hetzner Cloud cx33 is an x86_64 virtual server utilizing the QEMU hypervisor and powered by 4 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 8.0 GB of RAM, 80 GB of local SSD storage, and no bundled GPUs. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core performance in stress-ng tests, while multi-core CPU capabilities, memory bandwidth, and static web serving throughput remain average. Tradeoffs include a shared CPU model and weaker performance in software compilation and PassMark memory tests. Qualitatively, the server provides a cost-effective option for resource-dense, low-cost workloads. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic database tasks, and general-purpose development environments.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cx33 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local SSD storage, delivering strong single-core and average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cx33 is an x86_64 virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 80 GB SSD storage, suitable for lightweight workloads.
tagline: An affordable x86_64 virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and local SSD storage for lightweight multi-core workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
8.0 GB RAM and 80 GB local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Average static web serving and database performance
Appropriate for lightweight web servers and development environments
